name: fellows-private-data-ops
description: Lets Claude read your saved groups, group members, and notes you've added. This data is private to you — when Claude reads it, it goes to Anthropic's cloud. Skip this extension if you'd rather Claude only see the public fellows directory.
long_description: Part of the Fellows Directory integration with Claude Desktop. Reads relationships.db (your saved groups) — exported from the Fellows app's Settings → Download a backup. Joins to a bundled snapshot of fellows.db so group-member queries return names and emails in one round-trip without a second extension hop. Both DBs are opened read-only at the SQLite layer (readOnly:true on relationships.db; mode=ro URI ATTACH on fellows.db). Architectural posture: spec axis pick mcp-exposure:shared+private+comms, the Private half. AC-MCP-A applies: cloud AI clients see your group data when they use this — the PWA's setup screen surfaces that boundary up front so it's an informed choice. v1 does not implement a per-call consent gate; the boundary is documented + the user picks.

relationships_db.title: Your groups database
relationships_db.description: Path to relationships.db — export it from the Fellows app: Settings → Download a backup. The file usually lands in ~/Downloads.

tools.name: list_groups
tools.description: List all groups in your Private DB, newest-touched first.

tools.name: find_group
tools.description: Find groups whose name contains the given substring (case-insensitive).

tools.name: get_group_members
tools.description: Fetch one group plus its members, joined to fellows.db for names + emails.
